before_code: Polynomial.splits_of_splits_of_dvd (algebraMap (ZMod p) K) hf0 hg this
description: Polynomial.Splits became a unary predicate over the polynomial directly. Old `Splits i f` (binary, with ringhom) is now `Splits (f.map i)` (unary). All call sites of `splits_of_splits_of_dvd` and similar must move from binary to unary form, applying `Polynomial.map_dvd` to lift divisibility through the ring hom.
Module.card_eq_pow_finrank moved from root namespace to Module namespace.
The Irreducible structure field was renamed from not_unit to not_isUnit (matching IsUnit convention).
With Splits being unary, `splits_id_iff_splits` is no longer needed. `IsAlgClosed.splits` directly returns `Splits p`, no ring-hom-id rewriting needed.

`List.le_maximum_of_mem'` directly returns `(a : WithBot α) ≤ maximum l` requiring only the membership proof. The two-argument `le_maximum_of_mem` (which required an explicit equality `maximum l = m`) is harder to use; the primed version is preferred when the maximum form is what we need.

When mathlib and doc-gen4 share a transitive dependency (Cli) at different versions, put `require mathlib` last in lakefile.toml so Mathlib's pinned versions win and `lake exe cache get` works correctly. Otherwise the cache hash computation breaks.

before_code:
import Mathlib.NumberTheory.ModularForms.LevelOne.SturmBound
-- apply ModularForm.sturm_bound_levelOne; intro i hi (coefficient-vanishing form)
after_code:
import Mathlib.NumberTheory.ModularForms.LevelOne.DimensionFormula
-- sturm_bound_levelOne now takes order hyp; bridge via PowerSeries.nat_le_order
description: Bumping LeanBridge from the personal fork branch CBirkbeck/mathlib4@combined-e4e6-sturm to official mathlib v4.31.0: the fork's LevelOne/SturmBound.lean and DimensionFormula.lean were upstreamed, but merged INTO DimensionFormula.lean (no separate SturmBound module). `ModularForm.sturm_bound_levelOne` changed from the coefficient-vanishing hypothesis (∀ i, 12i≤k → coeff i = 0) to an ORDER hypothesis (↑(k.toNat/12) < (qExpansion 1 f).order) with f implicit. Project's eq_of_sturm_bound bridged via PowerSeries.nat_le_order plus a k<0 branch using qExpansion_zero/order_zero/ENat.coe_lt_top.

On v4.31.0, `convert` over IsCoprime/polynomial goals emits an extra leading instance-equality congruence goal (e.g. `commSemiring = CommRing.toCommSemiring`) that older mathlib closed automatically. Fix by discharging it with a `· rfl` bullet before the intended goal. Trailing `convert C.hgcd ...` similarly left a `↑(Fin.castSucc ⟨m,_⟩) = m` goal closed with `<;> simp [Fin.coe_castSucc]`.

Migrated LeanBridge's 391-knowl LMFDB LaTeX blueprint to a standalone verso-blueprint (blueprint-verso/, package LeanBridgeBlueprint, pinned VersoBlueprint@v4.31.0 matching the project toolchain). Knowls are informal (no Lean refs) so the blueprint imports no mathlib -> light, decoupled build. Done with a deterministic converter (scripts/convert_knowls.py), not LLM workers, since the 391 entries are uniform.

after_code: *bold* / _emph_; tex_prelude as top-level command emitting \def (not \newcommand); KaTeX-safe
description: Verso/verso-blueprint gotchas: (1) bold is *...* and emphasis _..._, NOT Markdown **/*; (2) tex_prelude is a top-level COMMAND (accumulates via imports), not a doc-body block; (3) KaTeX has builtins \R \C so prelude must use \def not \newcommand; (4) \unicode unsupported in KaTeX (use \text{}); (5) labels may contain dots; (6) literal []{}*_ and backticks in prose must be escaped/converted (tokenise generated markup first so only literals remain); (7) LMFDB knowls mix in {{KNOWL(..)}} macros, LaTeX accents, nested \cite braces, Markdown links, and $..$ nested in \text{}.
